free_text: (d,6Li) reaction on 24Mg and 26Mg at Ed=80 MeV.
The experiments were performed using
conventional dE-E surface barrier detector techniques.
Two to three telescopes were used during the
various experiments; the thicknesses of the dE and E
detectors were 100-200 mu-m and 1000-2000 mu-m,
respectively.
The incident beam was continuously monitored by a
Ge(Li) detector at an angle of 30 deg. lab relative to
the beam direction.
Self-supporting foils were used as targets with the
target thicknesses between 370 and 775 mu-g/cm2. The
isotopical enrichment of 24Mg and 26Mg was 99.96% and
97.10%, respectively.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Error from the graphic bars
The absolute cross sections were determined by the
integrated charge only and are believed to be accurate
to within 15%, except for the cases where the
uncertainties arising from counting statistics and
background subtraction are larger.
